items.hook: The Lewis Walpole Library in Farmington, Connecticut, is part of the Yale University Library system.
items.body: It holds important collections of 18th-century British literary remains, including an unrivalled quantity of Horace Walpole's papers and effects from his estate at Strawberry Hill in Twickenham in west London. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Polish–Lithuanian–Teutonic War: Battle of Grunwald: The allied forces of the Kingdom of Poland and the Grand Duchy of Lithuania defeat the army of the Teutonic Order.
items.body: The Polish–Lithuanian–Teutonic War, also known as the Great Teutonic War, occurred between 1409 and 1411 between the Teutonic Knights and the allied Kingdom of Poland and Grand Duchy of Lithuania. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: ‘People are picking the dumbest fights’: the tortured history of America’s culture wars
items.body:
In a new book, Isaac Butler goes back to the 1980s to trace how battles started against the arts, from Piss Christ to Mapplethorpe, and looks at what we can learn for today

Isaac Butl

items.hook: First Crusade: Christian soldiers take the Church of the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem after the final assault of a difficult siege.
items.body: The First Crusade (1096–1099) was the first of a series of religious wars, or Crusades, which were initiated, supported and at times directed by the Latin Church in the Middle Ages. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The United Red Army is founded in Japan.
items.body: The United Red Army (URA) was a far-left militant organization that operated in Japan between July 1971 and March 1972. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Jean Brown was born in Brooklyn, New York, and is best known for her work as a librarian and art collector.

items.hook: Mary Wings was an American cartoonist, writer, and artist.

items.hook: In his book Clinical Psychiatry, Emil Kraepelin gives a name to Alzheimer's disease, naming it after his colleague Alois Alzheimer.

items.hook: Sir Edward Waterhouse (1535–1591) was an English-born administrator in Ireland during the reign of Queen Elizabeth I.
items.body: He was the first to hold the office of Chief Secretary for Ireland, and served as Chancellor of the Exchequer of Ireland from 1586 to 1589. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: With 19.4 million passengers and 205,086 aircraft movements in 2025, Calgary International is the busiest airport in Alberta and the fourth-busiest in Canada by both passenger traffic and aircraft movements.
